test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A modern statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that targets the JVM and provides full interoperability with Java code.
2. Dart (2011) - A class-based object-oriented language created by Google for building web, server, and mobile applications, notably used in the Flutter framework.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional programming language built atop the Erlang BEAM VM that prioritizes scalability, concurrency, and developer productivity for distributed systems.
4. Rust (2012) - A systems-level programming language that guarantees memory safety and thread safety at compile time while delivering high performance without requiring a garbage collector.
5. Julia (2012) - A dynamic language tailored for high-performance scientific and technical computing, combining the ease of use of Python with the speed of C.
6. TypeScript (2012) - Microsoft's open-source superset of JavaScript that introduces static typing, interfaces, and other features to improve development of large-scale applications.
7. Swift (2014) - Apple's modern programming language designed as a safer and more expressive replacement for Objective-C in iOS, macOS, and other Apple platform development.
